root_text.sn40.7:1.4: ‘idha bhikkhu sabbaso viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ samatikkamma natthi kiñcīti ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.sn40.7:1.6: So khvāhaṁ, āvuso, sabbaso viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ samatikkamma natthi kiñcīti ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ upasampajja viharāmi.
root_text.sn40.7:1.7: Tassa mayhaṁ, āvuso, iminā vihārena viharato viññāṇañcāyatanasahagatā saññāmanasikārā samudācaranti.
root_text.sn40.7:2.1: Atha kho maṁ, āvuso, bhagavā iddhiyā upasaṅkamitvā etadavoca:
root_text.sn40.7:2.3: Mā, brāhmaṇa, ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ pamādo, ākiñcaññāyatane cittaṁ saṇṭhapehi, ākiñcaññāyatane cittaṁ ekodiṁ karohi, ākiñcaññāyatane cittaṁ samādahā’ti.
root_text.sn40.7:2.4: So khvāhaṁ, āvuso, aparena samayena sabbaso viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ samatikkamma natthi kiñcīti ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ upasampajja vihāsiṁ.
root_text.sn40.7:2.5: Yañhi taṁ, āvuso, sammā vadamāno vadeyya …pe… mahābhiññataṁ patto”ti.

translation_text.sn40.7:0.3: A Question About the Dimension of Nothingness
translation_text.sn40.7:1.1: “They speak of this thing called the ‘dimension of nothingness’.
translation_text.sn40.7:1.2: What is the dimension of nothingness?
translation_text.sn40.7:1.4: ‘It’s when a mendicant, going totally beyond the dimension of infinite consciousness, aware that “there is nothing at all”, enters and remains in the dimension of nothingness.
translation_text.sn40.7:1.5: This is called the dimension of nothingness.’
translation_text.sn40.7:1.6: And so … I was entering and remaining in the dimension of nothingness.
translation_text.sn40.7:1.7: While I was in that meditation, perception and focus accompanied by the dimension of infinite consciousness beset me.
translation_text.sn40.7:2.1: Then the Buddha came up to me with his psychic power and said,
translation_text.sn40.7:2.3: Don’t neglect the dimension of nothingness, brahmin! Settle your mind in the dimension of nothingness; unify your mind and immerse it in the dimension of nothingness.’
translation_text.sn40.7:2.4: And so, after some time … I entered and remained in the dimension of nothingness.
translation_text.sn40.7:2.5: So if anyone should be rightly called a disciple who attained to great direct knowledge with help from the Teacher, it’s me.”
